title: ProjectedTextureMapControl Object
description: Provides access to the various settings that control how a projected texture is applied to a body.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: reset
methods.description: Resets the texture map back to its original default settings.

properties.name: isCapped
properties.description: When a cylindrical projected texture map is being used this property gets and sets if a cap is use for the cylindrical projection. This property is only valid in the case when the projectedTextureMapType returns CylindricalTextureMapProjection. The value of this property should be ignored in all other cases and setting the property will have no effect.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: projectedTextureMapType
properties.description: Gets and sets how the texture map is being applied onto the body.

properties.name: transform
properties.description: Gets and sets the transform that defines the position and orientation of how the texture is projected onto the body. The Z axis of the transform corresponds to the axis that is specified in the user-interface and is the primary direction of the texture.

classType.title: ProjectedTextureMapControl.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

reset.title: ProjectedTextureMapControl.reset Method
reset.description: Resets the texture map back to its original default settings.
reset.return_description: Returns true if the reset was successful.

isCapped.title: ProjectedTextureMapControl.isCapped Property
isCapped.description: When a cylindrical projected texture map is being used this property gets and sets if a cap is use for the cylindrical projection. This property is only valid in the case when the projectedTextureMapType returns CylindricalTextureMapProjection. The value of this property should be ignored in all other cases and setting the property will have no effect.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = projectedTextureMapControl_var.isCapped

# Set the value of the property.
projectedTextureMapControl_var.isCapped = propertyValue

transform.description: Gets and sets the transform that defines the position and orientation of how the texture is projected onto the body. The Z axis of the transform corresponds to the axis that is specified in the user-interface and is the primary direction of the texture.
